name: Collado de Contreras and Ruta Teresiana — circular desde Fontiveros
name_original: Collado de Contreras y Ruta Teresiana — circular desde Fontiveros
region_intro: Hiking around Fontiveros offers routes through the rural setting of the La Moraña region in Ávila, Spain. The landscape is characterized by open, agricultural fields and generally flat terrain, typical of the Castilian plateau. Paths are often easy, providing opportunities for leisurely strolls with minimal elevation gain. Natural features like the Laguna Grande o de los Arenales offer peaceful settings for nature observation. Best hiking trails around Fontiveros The most popular hiking route is Castillo-Palacio de Castronuevo — circular por los Parajes de La Mora, a 9.3 miles (15.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 53 minutes to complete, exploring the rural landscapes of La Moraña. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castillo del Duque de Montellano — circular desde Muñomer del Peco, an easy 3.0 miles (4.9 km) path. This route offers views of the historic castle amidst the gentle countryside. Local hikers also love the Collado de Contreras and Ruta Teresiana — circular desde Fontiveros, a 8.8 miles (14.2 km) trail leading through gentle rural landscapes and sections of the historic Ruta Teresiana, often completed in about 3 hours 37 minutes. Hiking around Fontiveros is defined by open fields, agricultural landscapes, and gentle paths across the Castilian plateau scenery. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 4 hikers have used komoot to explore Fontiveros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Fontiveros?
A: There are 5 hiking routes available around Fontiveros, offering a range of options from easy strolls to moderate walks across the Castilian plateau.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for hiking near Fontiveros?
A: The terrain around Fontiveros is generally flat and characterized by open, agricultural fields, typical of the Castilian plateau. You can expect gentle paths with minimal elevation gain, making it suitable for leisurely walks rather than strenuous trekking.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners in Fontiveros?
A: Yes, Fontiveros offers several easy routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed walk. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249233287/castillo-del-duque-de-montellano-circular-desde-munomer-del-peco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo del Duque de Montellano — circular desde Muñomer del Peco&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.0-mile (4.9 km) path with very little elevation change.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Fontiveros area?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Fontiveros are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146218436/castillo-palacio-de-castronuevo-circular-por-los-parajes-de-la-mora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo-Palacio de Castronuevo — circular por los Parajes de La Mora&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular trail exploring the rural landscapes.

Q: What kind of landmarks or points of interest can I expect to see on hikes around Fontiveros?
A: While hiking around Fontiveros, you'll encounter historical and cultural landmarks rather than dramatic natural features. You might pass by sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3704019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Narros de Saldueña&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5768238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castronuevo Castle-Palace&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3692892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Castronuevo&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features significant religious buildings such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5595980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of Fontiveros&lt;/a&gt;, where Saint John of the Cross was baptized.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Fontiveros suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, the generally flat and easy terrain makes many of the trails around Fontiveros suitable for families with children. Routes with minimal elevation gain and shorter distances are ideal for a family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails near Fontiveros?
A: Most trails in the rural, open areas around Fontiveros are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural land or near livestock, and to respect local regulations.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Fontiveros?
A: The best time to hike in Fontiveros is typically during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot on the open Castilian plateau, and winters can be cold, though snow is less common.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Fontiveros?
A: Public transport options directly to specific hiking trailheads in Fontiveros are limited. It is generally recommended to use a personal vehicle to access the starting points of the routes, especially those outside the immediate town center.

Q: Are there parking facilities available at the trailheads?
A: While dedicated trailhead parking lots might not be common for all routes, you can usually find roadside parking or parking within the small villages that serve as starting points for many of the trails. Always ensure you park responsibly and do not obstruct local access.

Q: What are some longer hiking options for more experienced hikers in the region?
A: For those looking for longer walks, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1256378787/collado-de-contreras-and-ruta-teresiana-circular-desde-fontiveros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Contreras and Ruta Teresiana — circular desde Fontiveros&lt;/a&gt; offer a moderate 8.8-mile (14.2 km) journey through rural landscapes, including sections of the historic Ruta Teresiana.

Q: Are there opportunities for hiking in nearby, more mountainous regions like the Sierra de Gredos?
A: While Fontiveros itself is flat, the broader Province of Ávila does offer more challenging and mountainous hiking opportunities, notably in the Sierra de Gredos. This range, located further south, provides a stark contrast with rugged mountains, deep valleys, and varied ecosystems for serious hikers, though it requires a drive from Fontiveros.

Q: What do other hikers say about their experience in Fontiveros?
A: Hikers often appreciate the peaceful, open landscapes and the opportunity for quiet, reflective walks through the agricultural heartland of La Moraña. The historical context, particularly the Ruta Teresiana, also adds a unique cultural dimension to the hiking experience.
tips:
In the square that has the same name, you can see this statue of San Juan de la Cruz, from the beginning of the s. XX as a commemoration of the 200th anniversary of the canonization of the saint, a native of this town.
In the same square you can also see the Hermitage of Nuestra Señora de los Mártires.

Beautiful church that is located on the outskirts of the town of Fontiveros and whose characteristic is that it is made of exposed masonry, an unusual fact in the ecclesiastical constructions of the area.

The church dates back to the time of the Catholic Monarchs, specifically the 16th century. Its main entrance presents a spectacular doorway attributed to the sculptor and architect Juan Rodríguez. This doorway follows the classic scheme, with a semicircular arch flanked by two columns with Corinthian capitals. On the entablature and triangular pediment, you will find granite sculptures representing mythological beings and gargoyles.
